accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text; contextual claims (SOR/TOR grant names, FDA approval) are verified accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; statements track the bill text and accurate real-world context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill ensures references to opioid overdose reversal drugs are not limited to naloxone.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the 21st Century Cures Act and Public Health Service Act programs whose references must be updated.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the 'as appropriate' nature and that the update applies only to the two named grant programs' pre-enactment references.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states the one-year deadline from enactment for updating existing references.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures both the forward-looking mandate and the one-year update requirement for the two specified grant programs.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent across its three paragraphs.

concise.why: Focused with minimal padding for a short bill summary.

correct_entities.why: HHS, FDA, the SOR/TOR Grants, Cures Act, and PHS Act are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The only figure stated, the one-year update deadline, matches the bill text.
